explanation: Runs at 9:00 AM from Monday through Friday.
Runs every 15 minutes, every hour, every day (at :00, :15, :30, :45 past each hour).

description: First day of every month at midnight
explanation: Runs at midnight (00:00) on the 1st day of every month.
Runs at 00:00, 06:00, 12:00, and 18:00 every day (every 6 hours starting from midnight).
Runs at 5:00 PM (17:00) from Monday through Friday.

description: Twice a day at 8 AM and 8 PM
explanation: Runs twice daily at 8:00 AM (08:00) and 8:00 PM (20:00).

description: Every quarter hour during business hours (9 AM to 5 PM) on weekdays
explanation: Runs every 15 minutes (:00, :15, :30, :45) from 9:00 AM through 5:45 PM on weekdays (Monday–Friday). Note: this is the standard idiom — it includes ticks at 17:15, 17:30, and 17:45 which fall slightly after the 5 PM close.

description: Last working day of the month at 6 PM (approximate — use day 28)
explanation: Approximates the last working day by running at 6:00 PM (18:00) on the 28th of every month. Day 28 is used because it is guaranteed to exist in every month and is typically near month-end.

description: Every 5 minutes between 2 AM and 4 AM on Saturdays
explanation: Runs every 5 minutes from 2:00 AM through 3:55 AM on Saturdays (covers the 2 AM–4 AM maintenance window; the last fire is at 3:55 AM, just before 4 AM).
